4086	  \mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\), it asserts
4087	  \[
4088	    ((\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\,(h \circ f)).\mathrm{app}\,P).\mathrm{hom}
4089	      \;=\;
4090	      (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}_{a_X P} \;\mathbin{;}\;
4091	      h^{*}\bigl(((\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\,f).\mathrm{app}\,P).\mathrm{hom}\bigr) \;\mathbin{;}\;
4092	      ((\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\,h).\mathrm{app}\,
4093	        (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'_{f}\,P)).\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\;
4094	      a_Z.\mathrm{map}\bigl(
4095	        (\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp}\,\varphi'_{f}\,\varphi'_{h}).\mathrm{hom}_P
4096	      \bigr).
4097	  \]
4098	  It is proved by the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) mate calculus: both \(\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\)
4099	  isomorphisms are \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) of composite adjunctions, so transposing the
4100	  identity under the composite adjunction for \(h \circ f\) and evaluating the left-hand side
4101	  by the mate identity \(\mathtt{homEquiv\_leftAdjointUniq\_hom\_app}\) reduces it to a
4102	  unit-level identity; the two \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) factors are then transported across
4103	  the adjunction units --- the \(\delta\)-free twin of
4104	  \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}.
4105	
4106	  \emph{The reduced tail goal.} After peeling the leading pseudofunctoriality factor
4107	  \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}\), the remaining obligation is a unit
4108	  identity for the two-layer composite adjunction (presheaf pullback--pushforward adjunction
4109	  composed with the sheafification adjunction), isolated as the named sub-lemma
4110	  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback\_comp\_tail}\). Writing \(B_{\varphi}\) for the
4111	  sheaf-level composite adjunction of \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_app_unit_eta_general} and
4112	  \(\mathtt{PresheafPullback}_f\) for the presheaf-level pullback along \(f\), the assembly
4113	  proceeds in the following ordered steps, mirroring the already-closed
4114	  \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp} (whose proof is the \(\delta\)-free analog of this one,
4115	  exactly one sheafification layer down):
4116	  \begin{enumerate}
4117	    \item[(a)] \emph{Strip the outer identity wrapper.} The tail goal carries an outer
4118	      \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}(\mathbf{1})\) change-of-rings wrapper coming from the
4119	      identity ring map; this restriction-of-scalars-along-identity factor is removed first,
4120	      so that the genuine comparison factors are exposed.
4121	    \item[(b)] \emph{Distribute the right-hand sheaf composite under \(\mathtt{forget}\).} The
4122	      right-hand side is the sheaf-level composite carrying the two sub-comparison factors
4123	      \(R_1\) (the \(f\)-layer) and \(R_5\) (the \(h\)-layer). Pushing the forgetful functor
4124	      \(\mathtt{forget}\) (sheaf \(\to\) presheaf) through the composite separates these two
4125	      factors \emph{without} disturbing the left-hand composite unit \(B_{h\circ f}.\mathrm{unit}\),
4126	      which is left intact for the final reassembly.
4127	    \item[(c)] \emph{Recover the sub-comparison units.} Apply
4128	      \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_app_unit_eta_general}
4129	      (\(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniqUnitEta\_app}\), the \(P\)-general step-1 brick) to rewrite
4130	      \(R_1\) as the composite-adjunction unit \(B_f.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,P\) and
4131	      \(R_5\) as \(B_h.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,(\mathtt{PresheafPullback}_f\,P)\). This is
4132	      precisely the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints characterisation
4133	      \(\mathtt{homEquiv\_leftAdjointUniq\_hom\_app}\), now used in its \(P\)-general form.
4134	    \item[(d)] \emph{Slide the \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) comparison past the recovered
4135	      units.} The sheaf-level comparison \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,h\,f\) is moved across the
4136	      two recovered units \(B_f.\mathrm{unit}\) and \(B_h.\mathrm{unit}\) by its naturality.
4137	    \item[(e)] \emph{Reassemble the composite unit.} Expanding
4138	      \(B_{h\circ f}.\mathrm{unit}\) by the composite-adjunction unit formula
4139	      \(\mathtt{comp\_unit\_app}\) and applying \(\mathtt{unit\_naturality}\) identifies the
4140	      slid expression of step~(d) with the left-hand composite unit, closing the tail.
4141	  \end{enumerate}
4142	
4143	  \emph{The precise binding obligation.} The one place where the assembly is not yet a
4144	  mechanical paste is the bridge between the \emph{presheaf}-level left-hand data (the
4145	  presheaf pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}^{\mathrm{pre}}\) and the composite unit
4146	  \(B_{h\circ f}.\mathrm{unit}\)) and the \emph{sheaf}-level right-hand factors \(R_1, R_5\)
4147	  wrapped in \(\mathtt{forget}\). Crossing this boundary requires the compatibility
4148	  \[
4149	    \mathtt{forget} \circ \mathtt{pushforward}^{\mathrm{sheaf}}
4150	      \;=\;
4151	    \mathtt{pushforward}^{\mathrm{pre}} \circ \mathtt{forget},
4152	  \]
4153	  interacting with the recovered \(B_f\)- and \(B_h\)-units of step~(c). This compatibility is
4154	  the natural unit to isolate as its own named sub-lemma \emph{before} the assembly: once it
4155	  is in hand, steps (a)--(e) become a mechanical paste, whereas without it the presheaf- and
4156	  sheaf-level pushforwards cannot be aligned at the recovered units. The remainder of the tail
4157	  is bookkeeping around this single compatibility.
4158	
4159	  This is the sheafification-laden analog of \cref{lem:pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}; unlike
4160	  that lemma it is not definitional sectionwise, because the adjunctions involved are
4161	  composite rather than single transposes. The outer Sq1 lemma
4162	  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback\_comp}\) \emph{consumes} this tail: it peels the leading
4163	  \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{inv}\) factor and then invokes
4164	  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback\_comp\_tail}\) for the residual unit identity.
4165	  \emph{Warning:} transposing the \emph{whole} tail back under the composite adjunction --- as
4166	  opposed to recovering the two sub-units individually via step~(c) and reassembling --- is
4167	  \emph{circular} (verified): it returns the very identity one set out to prove, so the
4168	  step-(c) recovery of the individual \(R_1, R_5\) units is essential.
4169	
4170	  \emph{Sq3 (unit-iso transport).} The monoidal-unit sheafification isomorphism
4171	  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) is carried through the same \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
4172	  identification used for Sq2.
4173	
4174	  \emph{Sq4 (the connecting iso).} The scheme-level composition coherence of
4175	  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) relates \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,(h \circ f)\,M\) to
4176	  \(h^{*}\bigl(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M\bigr)\),
4177	  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,h\,(f^{*}M)\), and the pseudofunctoriality component
4178	  \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_M\); it produces the final
4179	  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\bigl((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_M,
4180	  (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,h\,f)_N\bigr)\) factor. It is \emph{absent from Mathlib and not yet
4181	  built}; but \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) factors through \(\mathtt{sheafCompPb}\) (as
4182	  \(\mathtt{sheafCompPb}^{-1}\) followed by the pullback of a counit), so its composition
4183	  coherence reduces to that of Sq1 together with the pseudofunctoriality of the counit ---
4184	  i.e.\ Sq4 becomes a short corollary once Sq1 is in hand.
4185	
4186	  This Sq4 coherence is a \emph{standalone named obligation}, to be built separately and not
4187	  folded into Sq1: it is the \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) \emph{composition-coherence} sub-lemma
4188	  --- the naturality across the connecting-object boundary where the abstract pullback's
4189	  \(\mathtt{.val}\) presentation meets the helper-\(\mathtt{.obj}\) presentation --- that is
4190	  consumed inside \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap\_restrict}\). The prover should treat it as its own
4191	  brick (a corollary of Sq1, but with its own statement and its own connecting-object
4192	  bookkeeping), not as a step inlined in the proof of Sq1.
4193	
4194	  The two remaining ingredients are Sq1 (\(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback\_comp}\)) and,
4195	  downstream of it, Sq4: the monoidality Sq2b (\(\mathtt{pullbackComp\_}\delta\) together with
4196	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp\_lax\_}\mu\)) follows from the mate-calculus reduction above, the
4197	  Sq3 unit-iso transport follows from the same \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) identification, and
4198	  the Sq2 ring-map reconciliation is definitional. Sq4 reduces to Sq1
4199	  via the \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) factorisation, so Sq1 is the open target.
4200	  The adjunction-mate bridge \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\) (relating
4201	  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) for the left adjoints to \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) for the
4202	  right adjoints) is the device used in Sq2b and Sq4 to transport the pseudofunctoriality
4203	  coherence across the adjunctions.
4204	
4205	  The four squares do \emph{not} paste row-by-row. Because \(S_1^{h}\) acts on
4206	  \(\bigl((\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{obj}\,M\bigr) \otimes
4207	  \bigl((\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{obj}\,N\bigr)\), \emph{not} on
4208	  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'_{f}\,(M \otimes N)\), the factors must first be slid past one
4209	  another by \(\delta_{\mathrm{natural}}\) and the naturality of the intervening natural
4210	  transformations before assembly, exactly as in the D1$'$ naturality paste of
4211	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}. With Sq1 in hand, the Sq2 oplax decomposition, the
4212	  Sq3 unit-iso transport, and the Sq4 connecting-iso coherence assemble the four squares into
4213	  the displayed identity. The base-change-square consequence (the Remark) is then the
4214	  specialisation \(h := j'\) obtained by equating the two factorisations \(j' \mathbin{;} f =
4215	  g \mathbin{;} j\); the iff-clause is immediate, an isomorphism being preserved and reflected
4216	  by the pseudofunctoriality isomorphism it is conjugated by.
4217	\end{proof}

4219	\begin{lemma}
4220	[Pullback commutes with $\otimes$ on locally-trivial pairs (D4$'$)]
4221	  \label{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv}
4222	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackTensorIsoOfLocallyTrivial}
4223	  \uses{def:IsLocallyTrivial, lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
4224	        lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange,
4225	        lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
4226	        lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}
4227	  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and let
4228	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be \emph{locally trivial}
4229	  (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}). Then the sheaf-level comparison map
4230	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}} = \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) of
4231	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map} is an isomorphism:
4232	  \[
4233	    f^*(M \otimes_X N)
4234	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
4235	    (f^*M) \otimes_Y (f^*N),
4236	  \]
4237	  natural in \(M\) and \(N\). This is the comparison the relative Picard consumer needs;
4238	  it is established by promoting the already-built map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) to an
4239	  isomorphism through a chart-chase, with no comparison for arbitrary modules and no
4240	  filtered-colimit machinery.
4241	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
4242	  % \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} (pullback commutes with the tensor product,
4243	  % functorially) and Lemma \ref{lemma-pullback-locally-free} (the pullback of a locally
4244	  % free module is locally free --- the geometric content licensing the chart-chase)
4245	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2392-2400 and L2112-2118).
4246	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
4247	  % "\begin{lemma}
4248	  % \label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
4249	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
4250	  % a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$
4251	  % be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules. Then
4252	  % $f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{G})
4253	  % = f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{G}$
4254	  % functorially in $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$.
4255	  % \end{lemma}"
4256	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
4257	  % "\begin{lemma}
4258	  % \label{lemma-pullback-locally-free}
4259	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
4260	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces. If $\mathcal{G}$ is
4261	  % a locally free $\mathcal{O}_Y$-module, then
4262	  % $f^*\mathcal{G}$ is a locally free $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
4263	  % \end{lemma}"
4264	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemmas
4265	  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} and \texttt{lemma-pullback-locally-free}:
4266	  pullback commutes with \(\otimes\) functorially, and the pullback of a locally free
4267	  (locally trivial) module is again locally free; on locally-trivial pairs the comparison
4268	  is therefore an isomorphism, checkable on a trivialising cover.}
4269	\end{lemma}
4270	
4271	\begin{proof}
4272	  \uses{def:IsLocallyTrivial, lem:pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural,
4273	        lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange,
4274	        lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
4275	        lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback}
4276	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Lemmas \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} and
4277	  \texttt{lemma-pullback-locally-free}.}
4278	
4279	  By local triviality of \(M\) and \(N\) (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}) choose a common
4280	  affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both restrict to the trivial bundle,
4281	  \(M|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and \(N|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) --- the
4282	  common refinement of the two trivialising covers, exactly as in the proof that the
4283	  tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial
4284	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}). The preimages
4285	  \(\{f^{-1}U_i\}\) form an open cover of \(Y\). It suffices, by the
4286	  restriction-detects-isomorphism criterion \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, to show
4287	  that the canonical map \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) restricts to an isomorphism on
4288	  each \(f^{-1}U_i\); the canonical global map being shown locally-iso is exactly what
4289	  lets us conclude (a non-canonical patchwork of local isomorphisms would not glue).
4290	
4291	  Fix \(i\) and write \(g_i : f^{-1}U_i \to U_i\) for the restriction of \(f\), with open
4292	  immersions \(j_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) and \(j_i' : f^{-1}U_i \hookrightarrow Y\),
4293	  so \(f \circ j_i' = j_i \circ g_i\). The base-change coherence
4294	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange} identifies the restriction
4295	  \((j_i')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) with the comparison
4296	  \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) for \(g_i\) on the restricted modules,
4297	  and reduces the claim to: \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) is an
4298	  isomorphism. On \(U_i\) the restricted modules are trivial, \(j_i^*M \cong
4299	  \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and \(j_i^*N \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\); by naturality of
4300	  \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) in both arguments (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural})
4301	  these isomorphisms transport \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) to the
4302	  comparison on the unit pair \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(\mathcal{O}_{U_i},
4303	  \mathcal{O}_{U_i})\). That comparison is an isomorphism by
4304	  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit} --- the unit-pair case, where the structure-sheaf
4305	  comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\) (an isomorphism for all \(g_i\)) and the unitors
4306	  do the work. Hence \(\delta^{g_i}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(j_i^*M, j_i^*N)\) is an isomorphism,
4307	  so is its base-change identification \((j_i')^*\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\), and
4308	  thus \(\delta^{f}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) restricts to an isomorphism on each
4309	  \(f^{-1}U_i\). (Both sides are indeed locally trivial on this cover --- \(f^*M, f^*N,
4310	  f^*(M\otimes_X N)\) are locally trivial by \cref{lem:IsLocallyTrivial_pullback} and
4311	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} --- which is what makes the chart-local
4312	  comparison a map between copies of the structure sheaf.) By
4313	  \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}, \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}(M,N)\) is a global
4314	  isomorphism, natural in \(M, N\) by \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}.
4315	\end{proof}
